## The Region’s Trusted Immigration Lawyer

## Immigration Law Services in Dallas, Houston & Austin, TX
For decades, Foster LLP has continued to offer the very latest in immigration and naturalization services to individuals , employers , and investors throughout Texas, the United States and around the globe. Our experienced immigration lawyers understand the intricate nature of procuring temporary work visas, permanent residence, and citizenship. When you request a consultation with one of our immigration lawyers, a member of our dedicated team can answer any questions you may have about visa processing times, filing a green card application, U.S. citizenship requirements, as well as other matters related to the naturalization process.

## How Much Time Should You Expect Your Visa to Complete Processing?
The processing time for a United States visa application depends on many different factors, most notably the type of visa you are trying to get. While temporary nonimmigrant visas can take several weeks to process, an immigrant visa can take years to get through the approval process.
Since the United States government handles every applicant on a case-by-case basis, in the order that it is received, it can be difficult to give a definitive answer regarding your visa processing time. Your individual situation will impact your visa processing time; factors such as annual quota caps, your country of origin, and even your last name could play a role. While there are ways to expedite your request in certain situations, it’s important to understand the process so you know which options to take. During your consultation, we will examine your situation and decide on a course of action that best serves your individual needs.

## How to Minimize Your Visa Processing Time
If you’re applying for any sort of visa status in the United States, taking steps to streamline the processing time can help reduce delays and improve your peace of mind. While there is no way to completely control the process, there are steps you can take to ensure that things go as smoothly as possible, which can help you get more efficient results. Here are some tips to streamline and minimize your visa processing time:
- Complete the Application Correctly: Fill out the visa application form that applies to you. Be sure that you fill out every applicable part accurately and double-check for any errors or missing information before you submit your application. Incomplete or incorrect applications can lead to delays or even cause your application to be rejected, further complicating the process.
- Gather Documents: Every visa category will have different documentation requirements. Review your specific document checklist and gather everything you need, such as passport copies, photographs, financial statements, employment letters, and any other required paperwork. A little advance preparation will help streamline the application process and minimize your visa processing time.
- Pay the Fee: Make sure that you pay the required visa application fee and keep the payment receipt as proof; you may need it later during the application process.
- Visa Interview: For many visa categories, you’ll need to complete an interview at the U.S. embassy or consulate. If this applies to you, schedule your appointment as soon as you can – availability can vary, and you want to have your interview as soon as possible. Be prepared for the interview by reviewing the specific requirements for your visa category and by practicing your answers to potential interview questions. Arrive on time for your interview and make sure to bring any documentation that you might need.
- Monitor Your Progress: After the visa interview, you will be able to track your application’s status online. Stay proactive and regularly check for updates. If you notice any delays or issues, reach out to the appropriate office for clarification and assistance.
- Consider Alternative Options: In certain cases, such as employment-based visas, you may be eligible for premium processing options that speed up your visa processing time. These options usually involve an additional fee but can yield faster results if you need them.
- Seek Professional Assistance: The visa application process can be complex, frustrating, and overwhelming. Consulting an immigration law firm early in your process can make a big difference. We can guide you through each step of the process, help you understand the requirements, help you contact the appropriate parties when necessary, and ensure that your application is complete and error-free. If your application is delayed or rejected, your immigration attorney can help advocate for your interests to reach a favorable conclusion.
The visa application process for the United States can be long and complicated, so it’s important to start early and plan accordingly. By being well-prepared, organized, and proactive, you can streamline your visa processing time and increase your chances of a smooth and timely approval. Consider hiring an experienced professional to make the process easier and more efficient.

## How Hiring an Immigration Attorney Can Impact Your Visa Processing Time
Whether you’re filing for a visa or applying to expedite the process, hiring an immigration attorney can help streamline the experience. Hiring professional assistance can help reduce your visa processing time in a number of ways:
- Take Advantage of Our Experience: The immigration process is not always straightforward; having someone on your side who understands the various shortcuts, exceptions, and policy changes that come into play can help you stay ahead of any updates that might affect your visa processing time.
- Stay on Top of Deadlines: Applying for a visa or attempting to expedite an application can require a lot of paperwork. It can be difficult and overwhelming to stay on top of this process. When you work with an immigration attorney, you can trust that we will be aware of every deadline and make sure that all of the materials you need are correctly filled out and filed appropriately. This helps prevent delays and ensures that your visa is processed as quickly as possible.
- Save Time: We all lead busy lives; it can be difficult to devote the necessary time to things that are outside of our everyday routines. Our office will help you through every step, helping the process go more quickly so you can devote your energy to other things.
- Increase Your Chance of Success: If you are trying to expedite a visa, it’s worth noting that expedition requests are often denied, especially during busier times of the year. While the outcome is not guaranteed, hiring an attorney to help craft a compelling and well-documented case can help improve your chances for success.
- Achieve Peace of Mind: Wondering about your visa processing time can be incredibly stressful, particularly if you don’t know what to expect. Our office will keep you up to date every step of the way to help you feel better about the process.
When you work with Foster LLP, we will work hard to advocate for your interests and streamline your visa processing time as much as we can. Our team works with individuals, families, and commercial clients from throughout the Dallas, Austin, and Houston, TX areas. Contact our office today to schedule a consultation!
